heading: Grants for buses and bus facilities

children.label: A
children.text: the term “low or no emission vehicle” has the meaning given that term in subsection (c)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: the term “State” means a State of the United States; and

children.label: C
children.text: the term “territory” means the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the United States Virgin Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: to replace, rehabilitate, and purchase buses and related equipment, including technological changes or innovations to modify low or no emission vehicles or facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: to construct bus-related facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: section 5307 shall apply to recipients of grants made in urbanized areas under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: section 5311 shall apply to recipients of grants made in rural areas under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: designated recipients that allocate funds to fixed route bus operators; or

children.label: ii
children.text: State or local governmental entities that operate fixed route bus service.

children.heading: .—Subrecipients
children.label: B .—Subrecipients
children.text: A recipient that receives a grant under this subsection may allocate amounts of the grant to subrecipients that are public agencies or private nonprofit organizations engaged in public transportation.

children.heading: .—National distribution
children.label: A .—National distribution
children.text: $206,000,000 each fiscal year shall be allocated to all States and territories, with each State receiving $4,000,000 for each such fiscal year and each territory receiving $1,000,000 for each such fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Distribution using population and service factors
children.label: B .—Distribution using population and service factors
children.text: The remainder of the funds not otherwise distributed under subparagraph (A) shall be allocated pursuant to the formula set forth in section 5336 other than subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Transfer flexibility for national distribution funds
children.label: A .—Transfer flexibility for national distribution funds
children.text: The Governor of a State may transfer any part of the State’s apportionment under paragraph (5)(A) to supplement amounts apportioned to the State under section 5311(c) or amounts apportioned to urbanized areas under subsections (a) and (c) of section 5336.

children.heading: .—Transfer flexibility for population and service factors funds
children.label: B .—Transfer flexibility for population and service factors funds
children.text: The Governor of a State may expend in an urbanized area with a population of less than 200,000 any amounts apportioned under paragraph (5)(B) that are not allocated to designated recipients in urbanized areas with a population of 200,000 or more.

children.heading: .—Capital projects
children.label: A .—Capital projects
children.text: A grant for a capital project under this subsection shall be for 80 percent of the net capital costs of the project. A recipient of a grant under this subsection may provide additional local matching amounts.

children.label: i
children.text: in cash from non-Government sources other than revenues from providing public transportation services;

children.label: ii
children.text: from revenues derived from the sale of advertising and concessions;

children.label: iii
children.text: from an undistributed cash surplus, a replacement or depreciation cash fund or reserve, or new capital;

children.label: iv
children.text: from amounts received under a service agreement with a State or local social service agency or private social service organization; or

children.label: v
children.text: from revenues generated from value capture financing mechanisms.

children.heading: .—Period of availability to recipients
children.label: 8 .—Period of availability to recipients
children.text: Amounts made available under this subsection may be obligated by a recipient for 3 fiscal years after the fiscal year in which the amount is apportioned. Not later than 30 days after the end of the 3-fiscal-year period described in the preceding sentence, any amount that is not obligated on the last day of such period shall be added to the amount that may be apportioned under this subsection in the next fiscal year.

children.heading: Pilot program for cost-effective capital investment.—
children.label: 9 Pilot program for cost-effective capital investment.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2016 through 2020, the Secretary shall carry out a pilot program under which an eligible recipient (as described in paragraph (4)) in an urbanized area with population of not less than 200,000 and not more than 999,999 may elect to participate in a State pool in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: .—Purpose of state pools
children.label: B .—Purpose of state pools
children.text: The purpose of a State pool shall be to allow for transfers of formula grant funds made available under this subsection among the designated recipients participating in the State pool in a manner that supports the transit asset management plans of the designated recipients under section 5326.

children.heading: .—Requests for participation
children.label: C .—Requests for participation
children.text: A State, and eligible recipients in the State described in subparagraph (A), may submit to the Secretary a request for participation in the program under procedures to be established by the Secretary. An eligible recipient for a multistate area may participate in only 1 State pool.

children.heading: .—Allocations to participating states
children.label: D .—Allocations to participating states
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Secretary shall allocate to each State participating in the program the total amount of funds that otherwise would be allocated to the urbanized areas of the eligible recipients participating in the State’s pool for that fiscal year pursuant to the formulas referred to in paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Allocations to eligible recipients in state pools
children.label: E .—Allocations to eligible recipients in state pools
children.text: A State shall distribute the amount that is allocated to the State for a fiscal year under subparagraph (D) among the eligible recipients participating in the State’s pool in a manner that supports the transit asset management plans of the recipients under section 5326.

children.heading: .—Allocation plans
children.label: F .—Allocation plans
children.text: A State participating in the program shall develop an allocation plan for the period of fiscal years 2016 through 2020 to ensure that an eligible recipient participating in the State’s pool receives under the program an amount of funds that equals the amount of funds that would have otherwise been available to the eligible recipient for that period pursuant to the formulas referred to in paragraph (5).

children.heading: .—Grants
children.label: G .—Grants
children.text: The Secretary shall make grants under this subsection for a fiscal year to an eligible recipient participating in a State pool following notification by the State of the allocation amount determined under subparagraph (E).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Eligible recipients and subrecipients under this subsection should, to the extent practicable, seek to utilize the procurement tools authorized under section 3019 of the FAST Act ( note; ).49 U.S.C. 5325Public Law 114–94

children.heading: .—Written explanation
children.label: B .—Written explanation
children.text: If an eligible recipient or subrecipient under this subsection purchases less than 5 buses through a standalone procurement, the eligible recipient or subrecipient shall provide to the Secretary a written explanation regarding why the tools authorized under section 3019 of the FAST Act ( note; ) were not utilized.49 U.S.C. 5325Public Law 114–94

children.heading: Buses and Bus Facilities Competitive Grants.—
children.label: b Buses and Bus Facilities Competitive Grants.—
A
children.text: replacing, rehabilitating, purchasing, or leasing buses or related equipment; and

children.label: B
children.text: rehabilitating, purchasing, constructing, or leasing bus-related facilities.

children.heading: .—Grant considerations
children.label: 2 .—Grant considerations
children.text: In making grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall consider the age and condition of buses, bus fleets, related equipment, and bus-related facilities.

children.heading: .—Statewide applications
children.label: 3 .—Statewide applications
children.text: A State may submit a statewide application on behalf of a public agency or private nonprofit organization engaged in public transportation in rural areas or other areas for which the State allocates funds. The submission of a statewide application shall not preclude the submission and consideration of any application under this subsection from other eligible recipients (as described in subsection (a)(4)) in an urbanized area in a State.

children.label: A
children.text: disclose all metrics and evaluation procedures to be used in considering grant applications under this subsection upon issuance of the notice of funding availability in the Federal Register; and

children.label: B
children.text: publish a summary of final scores for selected projects, metrics, and other evaluations used in awarding grants under this subsection in the Federal Register.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), not less than 15 percent of the amounts made available under this subsection in a fiscal year shall be distributed to projects in rural areas.

children.heading: .—Unutilized amounts
children.label: B .—Unutilized amounts
children.text: The Secretary may use less than 15 percent of the amounts made available under this subsection in a fiscal year for the projects described in subparagraph (A) if the Secretary cannot meet the requirement of that subparagraph due to insufficient eligible applications.

children.label: i
children.text: section 5307 for eligible recipients of grants made in urbanized areas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: section 5311 for eligible recipients of grants made in rural areas.

children.heading: .—Government share of costs
children.label: B .—Government share of costs
children.text: The Government share of the cost of an eligible project carried out under this subsection shall not exceed 80 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: shall remain available for 3 fiscal years after the fiscal year for which the amount is made available; and

children.label: B
children.text: that remain unobligated at the end of the period described in subparagraph (A) shall be added to the amount made available to an eligible project in the following fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 8 .—Limitation
children.text: Of the amounts made available under this subsection, not more than 10 percent may be awarded to a single grantee.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than 30 days after the date on which amounts are made available for obligation under this subsection for a full fiscal year, solicit grant applications for eligible projects on a competitive basis; and

children.label: i
children.text: 75 days after the date on which the solicitation expires; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the end of the fiscal year in which the Secretary solicited the grant applications.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: An eligible recipient of a grant under this subsection may submit an application in partnership with other entities, including a transit vehicle manufacturer that intends to participate in the implementation of a project under this subsection and subsection (c).

children.heading: .—Competitive procurement
children.label: B .—Competitive procurement
children.text: Projects awarded with partnerships under this subsection shall be considered to satisfy the requirement for a competitive procurement under section 5325.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Eligible recipients under this subsection should, to the extent practicable, seek to utilize the procurement tools authorized under section 3019 of the FAST Act ( note; ).49 U.S.C. 5325Public Law 114–94

children.heading: .—Written explanation
children.label: B .—Written explanation
children.text: If an eligible recipient under this subsection purchases less than 5 buses through a standalone procurement, the eligible recipient shall provide to the Secretary a written explanation regarding why the tools authorized under section 3019 of the FAST Act ( note; ) were not utilized.49 U.S.C. 5325Public Law 114–94

children.heading: Low or No Emission Grants.—
children.label: c Low or No Emission Grants.—
A
children.text: the term “direct carbon emissions” means the quantity of direct greenhouse gas emissions from a vehicle, as determined by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency;

children.label: i
children.text: acquiring low or no emission vehicles;

children.label: ii
children.text: leasing low or no emission vehicles;

children.label: iii
children.text: acquiring low or no emission vehicles with a leased power source;

children.label: iv
children.text: constructing facilities and related equipment for low or no emission vehicles;

children.label: v
children.text: leasing facilities and related equipment for low or no emission vehicles;

children.label: vi
children.text: constructing new public transportation facilities to accommodate low or no emission vehicles; or

children.label: vii
children.text: rehabilitating or improving existing public transportation facilities to accommodate low or no emission vehicles;

children.label: C
children.text: the term “leased power source” means a removable power source, as defined in subsection (c)(3) of section 3019 of the Federal Public Transportation Act of 2015 that is made available through a capital lease under such section;

children.label: D
children.text: the term “low or no emission bus” means a bus that is a low or no emission vehicle;

children.label: i
children.text: a passenger vehicle used to provide public transportation that the Secretary determines sufficiently reduces energy consumption or harmful emissions, including direct carbon emissions, when compared to a comparable standard vehicle; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a zero emission vehicle used to provide public transportation;

children.label: F
children.text: the term “recipient” means a designated recipient, a local governmental authority, or a State that receives a grant under this subsection for an eligible project; and

children.label: G
children.text: the term “zero emission vehicle” means a low or no emission vehicle that produces no carbon or particulate matter.

children.heading: .—General authority
children.label: 2 .—General authority
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to recipients to finance eligible projects under this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to eligible recipients in urbanized areas, section 5307; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to eligible recipients in rural areas, section 5311.

children.heading: .—Government share of costs for certain projects
children.label: B .—Government share of costs for certain projects
children.text: Section 5323(i) applies to eligible projects carried out under this subsection, unless the recipient requests a lower grant percentage.

children.heading: .—Combination permitted
children.label: i .—Combination permitted
children.text: An eligible project carried out under this subsection may receive funding under section 5307 or any other provision of law.

children.heading: .—Government share
children.label: ii .—Government share
children.text: Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed to alter the Government share required under paragraph (7), section 5307, or any other provision of law.

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrates a long-term fleet management plan with a strategy for how the applicant intends to use the current application and future acquisitions;

children.label: ii
children.text: addresses the availability of current and future resources to meet costs;

children.label: iii
children.text: considers policy and legislation impacting technologies;

children.label: iv
children.text: includes an evaluation of existing and future facilities and their relationship to the technology transition;

children.label: v
children.text: describes the partnership of the applicant with the utility or alternative fuel provider of the applicant; and

children.label: vi
children.text: examines the impact of the transition on the applicant’s current workforce by identifying skill gaps, training needs, and retraining needs of the existing workers of the applicant to operate and maintain zero emission vehicles and related infrastructure and avoids the displacement of the existing workforce.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than 30 days after the date on which amounts are made available for obligation under this subsection for a full fiscal year, solicit grant applications for eligible projects on a competitive basis; and

children.label: i
children.text: 75 days after the date on which the solicitation expires; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the end of the fiscal year in which the Secretary solicited the grant applications.

children.label: A
children.text: shall consider eligible projects relating to the acquisition or leasing of low or no emission buses or bus facilities that make greater reductions in energy consumption and harmful emissions, including direct carbon emissions, than comparable standard buses or other low or no emission buses; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall, for no less than 25 percent of the funds made available to carry out this subsection, only consider eligible projects related to the acquisition of low or no emission buses or bus facilities other than zero emission vehicles and related facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: shall remain available to an eligible project for 3 fiscal years after the fiscal year for which the amount is made available; and

children.label: B
children.text: that remain unobligated at the end of the period described in subparagraph (A) shall be added to the amount made available to an eligible project in the following fiscal year.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of an eligible project carried out under this subsection shall not exceed 80 percent.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: B .—Non-federal share
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of an eligible project carried out under this subsection may be derived from in-kind contributions.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A recipient of a grant under this subsection may submit an application in partnership with other entities, including a transit vehicle manufacturer, that intends to participate in the implementation of an eligible project under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Competitive procurement
children.label: B .—Competitive procurement
children.text: Eligible projects awarded with partnerships under this subsection shall be considered to satisfy the requirement for a competitive procurement under section 5325.

children.heading: .—Workforce Development Training Activities
children.label: d .—Workforce Development Training Activities
children.text: 5 percent of grants related to zero emissions vehicles (as defined in subsection (c)(1)) or related infrastructure under subsection (b) or (c) shall be used by recipients to fund workforce development training, as described in section 5314(b)(2) (including registered apprenticeships and other labor-management training programs) under the recipient’s plan to address the impact of the transition to zero emission vehicles on the applicant’s current workforce under subsection (c)(3)(D), unless the recipient certifies a smaller percentage is necessary to carry out that plan.
